chapter_context.summary: Addresses building a household, the path that seems right but ends in death, mercy to the poor, slow to wrath, and how righteousness exalts a nation.

outline.text: Building the House, Fear of the LORD, and Mocking at Sin
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Building the House, Fear of the LORD, and Mocking at Sin (14:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: The Bitter Heart, Deceptive Paths, and Simple vs. Prudent
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Bitter Heart, Deceptive Paths, and Simple vs. Prudent (14:10-18), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: The Poor vs. Rich, Mercy to Needy, and Strong Confidence
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Poor vs. Rich, Mercy to Needy, and Strong Confidence (14:19-27), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: A King's Honor, Slow to Wrath, and Righteousness Exalting
outline.explanation: This section focuses on A King's Honor, Slow to Wrath, and Righteousness Exalting (14:28-35), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

key_themes.title: Wisdom vs. Folly
key_themes.description: Proverbs outlines a practical blueprint for daily living, contrasting the actions, words, and consequences of the wise with those of the foolish.

key_themes.title: The Fear of the Lord
key_themes.description: The foundational principle of wisdom is a reverent awe and trust in God, which guides moral choices and shapes character.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the practical sayings of Chapter 14, what is one specific area of your speech, relationships, or work where you can apply divine wisdom today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose the path of integrity and diligence over convenience or compromise?

cultural_historical.title: Ancient Near Eastern Wisdom Literature
cultural_historical.text: Proverbs shares structural and thematic similarities with other ancient wisdom writings, but stands unique in placing the Fear of Yahweh as the foundation of all true wisdom.
